headline: Bernard Arnault, a 'free-marketeer' who whispers in the ear of every president
description: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (2/6). The LVMH chief has never hidden his allegiance to France's pro-business, establishment right. He's close to fellow billionaire Vincent Bolloré and, above all, pragmatic. Now he's preparing for the far right's rise to power.

description: Alongside the major issues discussed between the world's two leading powers, the fate of American citizens imprisoned in China on espionage charges has also become a bargaining chip.

headline: In southern Lebanon hospital, caregivers strive to overcome traumas of war
description: Doctors Without Borders provides individual counseling and group therapy sessions to professionals who have endured four months of conflict and an ongoing, unstable security situation.

headline: Climate change: Why the ECB refuses to create a lower interest rate for green investments
description: China's central bank guarantees low-cost funding for green investments. Its European counterpart, although a frontrunner on climate change, currently refuses to use this tool.

headline: Two months before Russian parliamentary elections, antiwar voices still struggle for traction
description: The Kremlin continues to stifle the few parliamentary candidates opposed to the war in Ukraine, even as the public and some industrialists begin to imagine peace.

headline: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
description: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.

headline: BTS electrifies Stade de France, seven years after their last Paris concert
description: The world tour of the industry's most powerful K-pop group wrapped up its European leg in Saint-Denis on July 17 and July 18.
